rationale: Filename version datetime and meta/dc:date reliably identify the whole-instrument open-data XML snapshot. Section @startPeriod is explicit but not safely mapped in this spike to a user-facing 'law in force on date X' promise. Narrative commencementNote is instrument-level only. Therefore the supported product promise is a version-to-version comparator (optionally labeled with source_version_datetime), not an as-at-date force-of-law query.

findings.notes: Acquisition timestamp only; never a legal version date.
Official data dictionary documents this as Version date for the XML resource. It is a whole-file version timestamp, not proven as provision commencement.

findings.source_locator: same as filename version date unless a distinct meta property is found
findings.notes: Treated as equivalent to source_version_datetime for these fixtures unless meta properties provide a different instrument version date.
Observed to align with filename version date (YYYY-MM-DD vs yyyymmddhhmiss). Treat as instrument snapshot/version date metadata, not provision commencement.

Explicit attribute present on many Cap. 614 sections. Attribute name is startPeriod, not effective_date or commencement_date. Without an authoritative mapping from the data dictionary/XSD semantics into a user-facing 'law in force on date X' promise, do not use this field for that claim in Stage B.

findings.source_locator: no dedicated last-updated field distinct from startPeriod observed
findings.notes: Do not infer from instrument version date or startPeriod.

findings.source_locator: no attribute literally named effective date on Cap. 614 sections
findings.notes: Must not relabel filename version date or startPeriod as effective_date without source documentation proving equivalence.

findings.source_locator: commencementNote narrative (e.g. '[30 June 2011]') at instrument level
findings.notes: Narrative commencementNote exists for Cap. 614 but is not a structured per-section commencement date suitable for as-at queries in Stage B.
